_note: RCD requirements are MANDATORY for the listed circuits — not optional. Any compliant design must include RCD protection where required.

principle.purpose: RCDs (Residual Current Devices) provide additional protection against direct contact (touching live conductors) and indirect contact (touching exposed-conductive-parts made live by fault). They detect imbalance between line and neutral currents indicating fault to earth.
principle.trip_threshold_30mA: Below the threshold for ventricular fibrillation. Trip time at IΔn ≤ 40 ms, at 5×IΔn ≤ 40 ms (BS EN 61008/61009).

circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.circuit: Socket outlets rated ≤ 32A intended for general use
circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.exception: Specific labelled sockets for stated equipment may be exempt with documented risk assessment (e.g. fridge in commercial kitchen, server PDU on UPS).

circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.circuit: Mobile equipment with current rating ≤ 32A for use outdoors
Final circuits supplying luminaires within domestic (household) premises
circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.exception: None — added in AMD 2 (2022). All domestic lighting circuits now require 30mA RCD.

circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.circuit: Cables installed in walls or partitions at depth ≤ 50mm without mechanical protection or earthed metallic covering
circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.exception: If cables in safe zones AND covered by 30mA RCD OR installed in earthed metallic conduit/SWA.

circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.circuit: Cables installed in walls or partitions containing metallic parts (steel studs, metallic ceiling grid)
circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.exception: Unless cable is in earthed metallic enclosure or has earthed metallic covering.

circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.circuit: Final circuits for EV charging points
circuits_requiring_30ma_rcd.exception: Type A or Type B RCD required depending on charging unit and DC fault current characteristics.

bathrooms_section_701._zones: Zone 0 (in bath/shower), Zone 1 (above bath), Zone 2 (1.2m beyond Zone 1)
bathrooms_section_701.rcd_required: 30mA RCD on ALL circuits supplying any location, including outside zones if in same room.

swimming_pools_section_702.rcd_required: 30mA RCD on all circuits supplying or in zone 0, 1, 2.

saunas_section_703.rcd_required: 30mA RCD on circuits in zones 1, 2, 3 — exception for water heater.

construction_sites_section_704.rcd_required: 30mA RCD on all socket outlets up to 32A. 500mA RCD on all final circuits.

agricultural_section_705.rcd_required: 30mA RCD on socket outlets ≤32A. 100mA delayed RCD on circuits supplying equipment.

marinas_section_709.rcd_required: 30mA RCD per socket outlet (one RCD per socket, max 4 sockets per RCD acceptable).

medical_locations_section_710._note: Group 1 and Group 2 medical locations require specialised protection (medical IT system in Group 2). RCD requirements are more nuanced.
medical_locations_section_710.rcd_required: Group 0: as normal. Group 1: 30mA RCD on socket outlets. Group 2: medical IT system (IT-M) with insulation monitoring, NO RCDs on patient-essential supplies.

ev_charging_section_722.rcd_required: Type A 30mA RCD per charging point AS A MINIMUM. Type B if EVSE does not have built-in 6mA DC fault detection.

AC.use: Older standard. Acceptable only for purely resistive loads. Not recommended for modern installations.

A.detects: AC + pulsating DC residual currents (rectified single-phase)
A.use: Standard for general use. Required for circuits with electronic loads (PCs, LED drivers, single-phase inverters).

F.detects: AC + pulsating DC + high-frequency residual currents
F.use: Single-phase inverter drives (washing machines, heat pumps with VFDs).

B.detects: AC + DC + high-frequency + smooth DC residual currents
B.use: Three-phase inverter drives, EV charging without built-in DC detection, solar PV inverters without integrated protection, UPS systems with rectifiers.

rcd_types.selection_rule: Match the RCD type to the load. Type AC RCD on an EV charger will fail to detect a DC fault — illegal under BS 7671. When in doubt, use Type A as minimum and Type B for any DC-capable load.

selectivity_between_rcds.principle: When RCDs are cascaded (e.g. 100mA at incomer + 30mA at final circuit), the upstream device must NOT trip when the downstream device trips. Achieved through both current ratio AND time delay.
selectivity_between_rcds.current_ratio: Upstream IΔn ≥ 3 × downstream IΔn (e.g. 100mA upstream / 30mA downstream gives 3.33:1 — OK)
selectivity_between_rcds.time_delay: Upstream must be S-type (time-delayed) — typically 40-130ms delay before trip. Downstream is instantaneous.
common_arrangements: 100mA S-type at MSB incomer + 30mA instantaneous RCBOs on final circuits
300mA S-type at submain + 30mA RCBO on socket circuits
500mA S-type at construction site origin + 30mA on socket outlets

rcd_vs_rcbo.RCD: Residual Current Device. Trips on earth fault only. Does NOT provide overcurrent protection. Used at consumer unit incomer or board incomer to protect groups of circuits.
rcd_vs_rcbo.RCBO: Residual Current Breaker with Overcurrent protection. Combines MCB + RCD in one device. Preferred for final circuits — provides discrete protection per circuit and isolates fault to one circuit only.
rcd_vs_rcbo.design_preference: Modern installations: RCBO per circuit. Avoids cascading trips and meets fault-isolation best practice. Cost ~3× MCB but justified.

common_pitfalls: Type AC RCD on circuit with electronic loads — will fail to detect DC component faults. Use Type A minimum.
Single 30mA RCD covering entire installation — nuisance trips, loss of all circuits on fault. Use RCBOs per circuit.
No RCD on lighting circuit in domestic post-AMD 2 — non-compliant since September 2022.
RCDs not selectivity-coordinated — upstream and downstream both trip, loss of unrelated circuits.
Missing 30mA RCD on outdoor sockets — frequent inspection finding.
Forgetting Type B RCD requirement for EV charging — major safety issue, non-compliance with Section 722.
